Phase A must add these named entries.

changes.check: Source cross-ref — Phase A: mEq/L equivalence wiring absent from units.yaml AND registry.py
changes.result: CONFIRMED GAP: units.yaml has no mEq/L named unit entry and no mEq/L↔mmol/L conversion block. registry.py _get_conversion_factor() has no sodium/potassium/chloride biomarker branch. _units_equivalent() (lines 89–94) only handles µmol/L variants. test_unit_registry.py line 61–63 (test_identity_for_biomarkers_without_conversion) currently passes sodium 140.0 mEq/L → mEq/L unchanged because sodium is NOT in _STRICT_CONVERSION_BIOMARKERS (lines 74–84) — registry falls through to passthrough. Phase A must add explicit mEq/L↔mmol/L 1:1 equivalence wiring, then add sodium/potassium/chloride to an appropriate equivalence group.

changes.check: Source cross-ref — Phase A: 10^9/L not defined in UnitEnum or units.yaml
changes.result: CONFIRMED ABSENT: UnitEnum (registry.py lines 42–59) has K_UL = 'K/μL' but no 10^9/L entry. units.yaml K_uL named unit (lines 45–49) references si_equivalent '10³/μL', not '10^9/L'. 10^9/L as a canonical unit token is absent. Phase A must define 10^9/L in units.yaml and add it to UnitEnum (or a string alias set) before registering K/μL ↔ K/uL ↔ 10^9/L equivalence.

changes.check: Source cross-ref — Phase C HbA1c inversion: multi-file breakage map
changes.result:
CONFIRMED: Flipping hba1c canonical from '%' to 'mmol/mol' requires coordinated changes across six locations:
(1) backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml line 196: unit '%' → 'mmol/mol'
(2) backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml lines 68–78: unit '%', bands 4.0–15.0 → mmol/mol equivalents (~31–104 mmol/mol)
(3) backend/core/units/registry.py lines 185–188 and 219–236: invert conversion direction (currently mmol/mol→'%'; must become '%'→'mmol/mol' or accept both with mmol/mol as base)
(4) backend/core/canonical/hba1c_layer_b_arbitration.py line 7 (docstring): update 'in %' to 'in mmol/mol'
(5) backend/tests/unit/test_unit_registry.py line 50: assert unit == '%' → assert unit == 'mmol/mol'
(6) backend/tests/unit/test_hba1c_governance.py line 42: currently asserts 48 mmol/mol → ~6.54% (NGSP); after flip must assert '%' input → mmol/mol output

changes.check: Source cross-ref — Phase C haematocrit inversion: multi-file breakage map
changes.result:
CONFIRMED: Flipping hematocrit canonical from '%' to 'L/L' requires:
(1) backend/ssot/biomarkers.yaml line 664: unit '%' → 'L/L'
(2) backend/ssot/scoring_policy.yaml lines 235–247: unit '%', bands 36–46 → L/L equivalents (~0.36–0.46)
(3) backend/core/units/registry.py lines 209–216: currently converts L/L→'%' (factor 100); must invert to '%'→'L/L' as base conversion
(4) backend/tests/regression/test_lc_s8_biomarker_unit_reference_incoherence_regression.py: tests at lines 146–167 per LC-S8C note §3.2 assume Layer A normalises TO '%'; all hematocrit assertions require replay after inversion.

changes.check: Source cross-ref — Phase C scoring band migration: glucose/lipids/creatinine test breakage
changes.result: CONFIRMED: backend/tests/unit/test_scoring_rules.py line 31 asserts glucose_rule.unit == 'mg/dL'. Phase C migrates glucose, total_cholesterol, ldl_cholesterol, hdl_cholesterol, triglycerides (all mg/dL per scoring_policy.yaml lines 55, 93, 110, 119, 133) and creatinine (mg/dL line 172) to UK/SI units. All affected test assertions in test_scoring_rules.py must be updated. Golden before/after test vectors are required per Phase C §Required work item 4.

changes.check: Source cross-ref — Phase B hemoglobin: factors present but uncited to primary source
changes.result: CONFIRMED: units.yaml lines 154–164 contain g_L_to_g_dL_hemoglobin (factor 0.1) and g_dL_to_g_L_hemoglobin (factor 10.0). LC-S8C pre-sprint note §2.4 confirms these factors exist but are 'not cited to UK lab primary source in LC-S8B'. Phase B for hemoglobin may proceed only if a UK primary-source citation (e.g. NHS/RCPCH lab handbook) is documented in docs/audit-papers/LC-S8D_uk_si_unit_governance_remediation_notes.md before the conversion is implemented. If absent: record PHASE_B_BLOCKED_PENDING_EVIDENCE.

changes.check: Source cross-ref — Phase B Ca/corrected_Ca/Mg/fT4: conversion factors absent from units.yaml
changes.result: CONFIRMED: units.yaml conversions sub-tree (lines 83–176) contains NO entries for calcium, corrected_calcium, magnesium, or free_t4. These biomarkers are not in _STRICT_CONVERSION_BIOMARKERS so registry passthrough applies — but Layer B cannot score them in a migrated unit without conversion. Phase B must record PHASE_B_BLOCKED_PENDING_EVIDENCE for all four unless conversion factors with primary-source citations are attached to the notes file.

changes.check: Source cross-ref — hba1c_pct KB package signals are out-of-scope for LC-S8D
changes.result: CONFIRMED: system_burden_registry.yaml line 314 registers hba1c_pct. LC-S8C note §3.1 identifies knowledge_bus/packages/pkg_kb52d_hba1c_pct_* as a residual signal evaluation risk. The allowed files list in the prompt does NOT include knowledge_bus/packages/. KB signal remapping cannot be executed in LC-S8D. Arbitration layer (hba1c_layer_b_arbitration.py) prevents numeric double-scoring on the ScoringRules path. Record KB signal remap as DEFERRED residual risk in notes file.

CONFIRMED ALL EXIST: backend/tests/unit/test_unit_registry.py (exists), backend/tests/unit/test_scoring_rules.py (exists), backend/tests/unit/test_hba1c_governance.py (exists), backend/tests/regression/test_lc_s8_biomarker_unit_reference_incoherence_regression.py (exists). All four test files referenced in Required validation commands are present — no creation needed, only updates.

changes.check: Forbidden files — verified absent from allowed scope
changes.result: CONFIRMED: run_work_package.py, golden_gate_local.py, update_cursor_status.py, latest_gate_evidence.json, latest_gate_output.txt, latest_cursor_status.json are not in the allowed files list. Tooling leakage paths (.codex/, .vscode/, AGENTS.md) explicitly prohibited.

CONFIRMED: change_type MIXED. Intelligence Core components affected: (a) unit normalisation in registry.py — base unit direction changes for hba1c and hematocrit; (b) scoring band values in scoring_policy.yaml — numeric ranges change for all Phase C markers; (c) output structure — scoring output unit labels change. Determinism: all changes are deterministic (lookup-based, no LLM). Output structure: same JSON keys, different unit/value fields. Ranking and ordering: stable (band-relative scoring unchanged). No hidden side effects identified beyond the six-file HbA1c coordination and four-file haematocrit coordination documented above.
